name: Silent Ping Monitor
description: A server status monitor gives identical responses regardless of input. Use time-based blind SQL injection with SLEEP() to extract the flag by measuring response delays.
briefing: IronGrid Systems runs a server health monitor that checks if a host ID exists in its database. The response is always the same generic status page — no difference between found and not found, and errors are fully suppressed. The only side channel available is time. A 'config' table is rumored to hold sensitive data. You'll need to use MySQL's SLEEP() function to create measurable delays that leak information one bit at a time.
objective: Use time-based blind SQL injection to extract the flag from the config table by measuring response times.
author: h4ck0rman
